title: Air Hockey Table — Puck Lift by Air Cushion and Rebound Dynamics
content: An air hockey table creates a thin air film (0.1–0.5 mm) through a perforated playing surface to levitate a puck, reducing friction coefficient to µ ≈ 0.0001 (from µ ≈ 0.4 dry). The puck slides freely, reflecting elastically from cushioned rails.
extended_content: Blower specifications: centrifugal fan, 200–400 Pa static pressure, 0.5–1.5 m³/min airflow. Hole pattern: 1.5–2 mm dia holes at 20–30 mm spacing, open area ratio 0.5–1.0%. Air layer thickness h ≈ Q/(A·v_exit); puck levitation requires gauge pressure P_gauge = F_puck/A_puck ≈ 0.2 N / 0.01 m² = 20 Pa. Puck rebound: angle of incidence ≈ angle of reflection (elastic); e = coefficient of restitution 0.85–0.95 for acrylic puck on acrylic surface. Score detection: IR photogate at goal slot detects puck passage. Motor drive: brushless 12 V centrifugal fan 40–80 W. Puck mass: 15–25 g; diameter 65 mm. Mallet mass: 25–40 g.
units: P: Pa, F: N, A: m², Q: m³/s, µ: dimensionless
source: Munson et al., Fundamentals of Fluid Mechanics, 8th ed.; Air Hockey International Rules; Khonsari & Booser, Applied Tribology
